name: Kaushitaki Upanishad
summary: The Kaushitaki Upanishad describes the path of the soul after death, the nature of prana as Brahman, and the dialogue between Indra and Pratardana. It teaches that prana (life-force) is consciousness and is Brahman.

verses.english_translation: 'Prana is Brahman' — thus indeed said Kaushitaki.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — मुख्यप्राणः — ब्रह्म — चैतन्यस्वरूपः। प्राणस्य ब्रह्मत्वम् उपदिश्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana — the chief life-force — is Brahman, whose nature is consciousness. This Upanishad teaches the identity of prana with Brahman, the ultimate reality.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे ह वा अमुं लोकं प्रेत्याभिसंभवन्ति | तान् चन्द्रमाः प्राणेन अनुप्रश्नं पृच्छति ||१.२||
verses.transliteration: sarve ha vā amuṃ lokaṃ pretyābhisaṃbhavanti | tān candramāḥ prāṇena anupraśnaṃ pṛcchati ||1.2||
verses.english_translation: All indeed, after death, go to that world (the moon). The moon questions them through prana.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मृत्योः परं सर्वे चन्द्रलोकं गच्छन्ति। चन्द्रमाः तान् प्रश्नं पृच्छति — कः असि इति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: After death, all souls go to the lunar world. The moon questions them: 'Who are you?' Those who know the Self pass through; those who do not know return to rebirth. This describes the two paths — the path of the gods (devayana) and the path of the ancestors (pitriyana).

verses.sanskrit: प्रज्ञया वाचं समारुह्य वाचा सर्वाणि नामान्याप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: prajñayā vācaṃ samāruhya vācā sarvāṇi nāmāny āpnoti ||3.8||
verses.english_translation: Having mounted speech through consciousness, one attains all names through speech.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्रज्ञा — चैतन्यम् — वाचः अधिष्ठाता। प्रज्ञया वाक् प्रवर्तते। एवं सर्वेन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Consciousness is the ground of speech. Speech functions because consciousness activates it. Similarly, all the senses and faculties are activated by consciousness alone. This points to consciousness (prajna) as the ultimate Brahman.

verses.transliteration: prāṇo'smi prajñātmā | taṃ mām āyur amṛtam ity upāssva ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: 'I am prana, whose Self is consciousness. Meditate on me as life and immortality.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — मुख्यप्राणः — प्रज्ञात्मा — चैतन्यस्वरूपः। माम् आयुः अमृतम् इति उपास्स्व।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Indra declares to Pratardana: 'I am prana, whose nature is consciousness. Meditate on me as life and immortality.' This identifies prana with Brahman — the conscious life-force that sustains all beings.

word_meanings.meaning: prana alone, whose Self is consciousness
having taken hold of this body

verses.english_translation: Prana alone, whose Self is consciousness, having taken hold of this body, raises it up.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — चैतन्यात्मा — इदम् शरीरम् परिगृह्य — उत्थापयति — जीवयति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana, whose nature is consciousness, animates the body. Without prana-consciousness, the body is inert. This is why prana is identified with Brahman — it is the conscious life-force that makes the body alive.

verses.sanskrit: ये वै के चास्माल्लोकात्प्रयन्ति चन्द्रमसमेव ते सर्वे गच्छन्ति ||१.२||
verses.transliteration: ye vai ke cāsmāl lokāt prayanti candramasam eva te sarve gacchanti ||1.2||
verses.english_translation: All who depart from this world go to the moon.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अस्मात् लोकात् प्रयन्ति — मृत्वा — सर्वे चन्द्रलोकम् गच्छन्ति। तत्र चन्द्रः प्रश्नं पृच्छति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: After death, all souls first go to the moon. There the moon questions them. Those who know the Self pass through to higher worlds; those who do not know return to rebirth on earth. This describes the two paths after death.

verses.sanskrit: स य एवं विद्वान् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानमेव पश्यति | सर्वेषु भूतेषु चात्मानम् ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: sa ya evaṃ vidvān sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmānam eva paśyati | sarveṣu bhūteṣu cātmānam ||3.1||

word_meanings.meaning: sees all beings as the Self

verses.english_translation: One who knows thus sees all beings as the Self alone, and the Self in all beings.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एवम् विद्वान् — प्राणम् ब्रह्म इति जानाति — सः सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानम् एव पश्यति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who knows prana as Brahman sees all beings as the Self and the Self in all beings. This is the vision of unity — the same teaching as the Isha Upanishad. Knowledge of Brahman leads to seeing the Self everywhere.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणो ह पिता प्राणो माता प्राणो भ्राता प्राणः स्वसा प्राण आचार्यः प्राणो ब्राह्मणः ||३.३||
verses.transliteration: prāṇo ha pitā prāṇo mātā prāṇo bhrātā prāṇaḥ svasā prāṇa ācāryaḥ prāṇo brāhmaṇaḥ ||3.3||
verses.english_translation: Prana is the father, prana is the mother, prana is the brother, prana is the sister, prana is the teacher, prana is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — सर्वस्य — पिता माता भ्राता स्वसा आचार्यः — ब्राह्मणः — ब्रह्मस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is identified with all relationships — father, mother, brother, sister, teacher — and ultimately with Brahman itself. All relationships are sustained by prana (consciousness). When prana departs, all relationships end. Therefore prana is the ultimate reality behind all bonds.

verses.sanskrit: यदि वाचि सर्वाणि नामान्यर्पयेत् | प्राणेन सर्वाणि नामान्याप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: yadi vāci sarvāṇi nāmāny arpayeta | prāṇena sarvāṇi nāmāny āpnoti ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: if one offers all names to speech
by prana one attains all names

verses.english_translation: If one offers all names to speech, by prana one attains all names.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: वाचि — सर्वाणि नामानि — अर्पयेत् — प्राणेन — सर्वाणि नामानि — आप्नोति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Speech is the vehicle of all names. By offering all names to speech and speech to prana, one attains all names through prana. This shows the hierarchy: prana is the ground of speech, and speech is the ground of all names and forms.

verses.sanskrit: प्रज्ञया वाचं समारुह्य वाचा सर्वाणि नामान्याप्नोति | प्रज्ञया चक्षुः समारुह्य चक्षुषा सर्वाणि रूपाण्याप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: prajñayā vācaṃ samāruhya vācā sarvāṇi nāmāny āpnoti | prajñayā cakṣuḥ samāruhya cakṣuṣā sarvāṇi rūpāṇy āpnoti ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: having mounted the eye through consciousness

verses.english_translation: Having mounted speech through consciousness, one attains all names through speech. Having mounted the eye through consciousness, one attains all forms through the eye.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्रज्ञा — चैतन्यम् — वाचः अधिष्ठाता। प्रज्ञया वाक् प्रवर्तते। एवं सर्वेन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Consciousness is the ground of speech. Speech functions because consciousness activates it. Similarly, all the senses and faculties are activated by consciousness alone. This points to consciousness (prajna) as the ultimate Brahman.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃ prāṇo'smi prajñātmā | taṃ mām āyur amṛtam ity upāssva ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: 'I am prana, whose Self is consciousness. Meditate on me as life and immortality.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अहम् — इन्द्रः — प्राणः अस्मि — प्रज्ञात्मा। माम् — आयुः अमृतम् — इति उपास्स्व।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Indra declares to Pratardana: 'I am prana, whose nature is consciousness. Meditate on me as life and immortality.' This identifies prana with Brahman — the conscious life-force that sustains all beings and leads to immortality.

verses.sanskrit: स य एवं विद्वान् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानमेव पश्यति | सर्वेषु भूतेषु चात्मानम् | न किञ्चन वेदनेन मन्यते ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: sa ya evaṃ vidvān sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmānam eva paśyati | sarveṣu bhūteṣu cātmānam | na kiñcana vedanena manyate ||3.1||

word_meanings.meaning: sees all beings as the Self
does not think anything through pain

verses.english_translation: One who knows thus sees all beings as the Self alone, and the Self in all beings. He does not think anything through pain.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एवम् विद्वान् — प्राणम् ब्रह्म इति जानाति — सः सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानम् एव पश्यति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who knows prana as Brahman sees all beings as the Self and the Self in all beings. This is the vision of unity — the same teaching as the Isha Upanishad. Knowledge of Brahman leads to seeing the Self everywhere, and this vision removes all suffering.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतर्दनो ह वै दैवोदासिरिन्द्रस्य प्रियं धामोपजगाम युद्धेन च पौरुषेण च ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: pratardano ha vai daivodāsir indrasya priyaṃ dhāmopajagāma yuddhena ca pauruṣeṇa ca ||3.1||
verses.english_translation: Pratardana, son of Divodasa, came to Indra's beloved abode by battle and valor.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्रतर्दनः — दिवोदासस्य पुत्रः — युद्धेन — पौरुषेण — इन्द्रलोकम् प्राप्तवान्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Pratardana reached Indra's heaven through his valor in battle. Indra, pleased with him, offered him a boon. Pratardana asked Indra to choose the boon himself — whatever would be most beneficial. Indra then taught him about prana as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तं होवाच वरं वृणीष्वेति | स होवाच त्वमेव वृणीष्व यं त्वं मनुष्याय हिततमं मन्यस इति ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: taṃ hovāca varaṃ vṛṇīṣveti | sa hovāca tvam eva vṛṇīṣva yaṃ tvaṃ manuṣyāya hitatamaṃ manyasa iti ||3.1||
verses.english_translation: He said to him: 'Choose a boon.' He said: 'You yourself choose — whatever you consider most beneficial for a person.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: इन्द्रः — वरम् वृणीष्व — इत्युक्तवान्। प्रतर्दनः — त्वम् एव वृणीष्व — यत् मनुष्याय हिततमम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Pratardana's response shows his wisdom — he asks Indra to choose the boon himself, trusting that Indra knows what is truly beneficial. Indra then teaches him about prana as Brahman — the most beneficial knowledge for any person.

verses.sanskrit: तं होवाच | मां विजानीहि | एतदेव मे मन्यस्व परमं हितमिति ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: taṃ hovāca | māṃ vijānīhi | etad eva me manyasva paramaṃ hitam iti ||3.1||

word_meanings.meaning: this alone is the highest benefit

verses.english_translation: He said to him: 'Know me. Consider this alone as the highest benefit.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: माम् विजानीहि — माम् — इन्द्रम् — प्राणम् — ब्रह्म — विजानीहि। एतत् परमम् हितम् — ब्रह्मज्ञानम् एव परमम् हितम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Indra says 'Know me' — know me as prana, as Brahman. This is the highest benefit for any person. All other boons — wealth, power, long life — are temporary. The knowledge of Brahman alone leads to immortality and is therefore the highest benefit.

verses.sanskrit: न वाचं विजिज्ञासीत वक्तारं विद्यात् | न गन्धं विजिज्ञासीत घ्रातारं विद्यात् ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: na vācaṃ vijijñāsīta vaktāraṃ vidyāt | na gandhaṃ vijijñāsīta ghrātāraṃ vidyāt ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: one should not inquire into speech
one should not inquire into smell

verses.english_translation: One should not inquire into speech — one should know the speaker. One should not inquire into smell — one should know the smeller.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: वाचम् न विजिज्ञासीत — वाक् विषयः न। वक्तारम् विद्यात् — वक्ता — आत्मा — विद्यात्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Do not inquire into the object (speech, smell, etc.) — inquire into the subject (the speaker, the smeller). The objects are finite and changing; the subject — the Self — is the eternal knower. This is the method of self-inquiry: turn attention from the object to the subject.

verses.sanskrit: न रूपं विजिज्ञासीत द्रष्टारं विद्यात् | न शब्दं विजिज्ञासीत श्रोतारं विद्यात् ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: na rūpaṃ vijijñāsīta draṣṭāraṃ vidyāt | na śabdaṃ vijijñāsīta śrotāraṃ vidyāt ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: one should not inquire into form
one should not inquire into sound

verses.english_translation: One should not inquire into form — one should know the seer. One should not inquire into sound — one should know the hearer.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: रूपम् न विजिज्ञासीत — द्रष्टारम् विद्यात् — आत्मानम् विद्यात्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Do not inquire into form — inquire into the seer. Do not inquire into sound — inquire into the hearer. The seer and hearer are the Self — the eternal witness behind all perception. This is the direct path to self-knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: न मनो विजिज्ञासीत मन्तारं विद्यात् | न बुद्धिं विजिज्ञासीत बोद्धारं विद्यात् ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: na mano vijijñāsīta mantāraṃ vidyāt | na buddhiṃ vijijñāsīta boddhāraṃ vidyāt ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: one should not inquire into mind
one should not inquire into intellect

verses.english_translation: One should not inquire into mind — one should know the thinker. One should not inquire into intellect — one should know the knower.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनः न विजिज्ञासीत — मन्तारम् विद्यात् — आत्मानम् विद्यात्। बुद्धिम् न विजिज्ञासीत — बोद्धारम् विद्यात्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Do not inquire into the mind — inquire into the thinker. Do not inquire into the intellect — inquire into the knower. The thinker and knower are the Self. This method of inquiry — turning from the instrument to the user of the instrument — leads directly to the Self.

verses.sanskrit: न आत्मानं विजिज्ञासीत आत्मानं विद्यात् | एष ब्रह्मलोकः सम्राट् इति होवाच इन्द्रः ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: na ātmānaṃ vijijñāsīta ātmānaṃ vidyāt | eṣa brahmaloko samrāṭ iti hovāca indraḥ ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: one should inquire into the Self
this is the world of Brahman

verses.english_translation: One should inquire into the Self — one should know the Self. This is the world of Brahman, O Emperor — thus said Indra.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आत्मानम् विजिज्ञासीत — आत्मानम् विद्यात् — एषः ब्रह्मलोकः — इन्द्रः उवाच।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The culmination of all inquiry is the Self. Know the Self — this is the world of Brahman. Indra tells Pratardana that the knowledge of the Self is the highest knowledge, and one who knows the Self lives in the world of Brahman even while in this body.

verses.sanskrit: स य एवं विद्वान् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानमेव पश्यति | सर्वेषु भूतेषु चात्मानम् | न किञ्चन वेदनेन मन्यते | न किञ्चन कर्मणा मन्यते ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: sa ya evaṃ vidvān sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmānam eva paśyati | sarveṣu bhūteṣu cātmānam | na kiñcana vedanena manyate | na kiñcana karmaṇā manyate ||3.1||

word_meanings.meaning: sees all beings as the Self
does not think anything through pain
does not think anything through action

verses.english_translation: One who knows thus sees all beings as the Self alone, and the Self in all beings. He does not think anything through pain; he does not think anything through action.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एवम् विद्वान् — सर्वाणि भूतानि — आत्मानम् एव पश्यति। न वेदनेन — न कर्मणा — किञ्चन मन्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The knower of Brahman sees all beings as the Self and the Self in all beings. He is not affected by pain or action — for he knows that the Self is beyond all pain and action. This is the state of liberation — seeing unity everywhere, unaffected by the pairs of opposites.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणो ह पिता प्राणो माता प्राणो भ्राता प्राणः स्वसा प्राण आचार्यः प्राणो ब्राह्मणः ||३.३||
verses.transliteration: prāṇo ha pitā prāṇo mātā prāṇo bhrātā prāṇaḥ svasā prāṇa ācāryaḥ prāṇo brāhmaṇaḥ ||3.3||
verses.english_translation: Prana is the father, prana is the mother, prana is the brother, prana is the sister, prana is the teacher, prana is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — सर्वस्य — पिता माता — ब्राह्मणः — ब्रह्मस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is identified with all relationships and ultimately with Brahman. All relationships are sustained by prana (consciousness). When prana departs, all relationships end.

verses.sanskrit: यदि वाचि सर्वाणि नामान्यर्पयेत् | प्राणेन सर्वाणि नामान्याप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: yadi vāci sarvāṇi nāmāny arpayeta | prāṇena sarvāṇi nāmāny āpnoti ||3.8||
verses.english_translation: If one offers all names to speech, by prana one attains all names.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: वाचि — सर्वाणि नामानि — अर्पयेत् — प्राणेन — सर्वाणि नामानि — आप्नोति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Speech is the vehicle of all names. By offering all names to speech and speech to prana, one attains all names through prana. Prana is the ground of speech.

verses.sanskrit: यदि चक्षुषि सर्वाणि रूपाण्यर्पयेत् | प्राणेन सर्वाणि रूपाण्याप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: yadi cakṣuṣi sarvāṇi rūpāṇy arpayeta | prāṇena sarvāṇi rūpāṇy āpnoti ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: offers all forms to the eye

verses.english_translation: If one offers all forms to the eye, by prana one attains all forms.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: चक्षुषि — सर्वाणि रूपाणि — अर्पयेत् — प्राणेन — सर्वाणि रूपाणि — आप्नोति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The eye is the vehicle of all forms. By offering all forms to the eye and the eye to prana, one attains all forms through prana. Prana is the ground of all perception.

verses.transliteration: yadi śrotre sarvāñ chabdān arpayeta | prāṇena sarvāñ chabdān āpnoti ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: offers all sounds to the ear

verses.english_translation: If one offers all sounds to the ear, by prana one attains all sounds.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: श्रोत्रे — सर्वान् शब्दान् — अर्पयेत् — प्राणेन — सर्वान् शब्दान् — आप्नोति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The ear is the vehicle of all sounds. By offering all sounds to the ear and the ear to prana, one attains all sounds through prana. Prana is the ground of all hearing.

verses.sanskrit: यदि मनसि सर्वान् कामानर्पयेत् | प्राणेन सर्वान् कामानाप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: yadi manasi sarvān kāmān arpayeta | prāṇena sarvān kāmān āpnoti ||3.8||

word_meanings.meaning: offers all desires to the mind

verses.english_translation: If one offers all desires to the mind, by prana one attains all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनसि — सर्वान् कामान् — अर्पयेत् — प्राणेन — सर्वान् कामान् — आप्नोति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The mind is the vehicle of all desires. By offering all desires to the mind and the mind to prana, one attains all desires through prana. Prana is the ground of all mental activity.

verses.sanskrit: स एष प्राणः प्रज्ञात्मा | आनन्दोऽजरोऽमृतः | न साधुना कर्मणा भूयान् नो एवासाधुना कनीयान् ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: sa eṣa prāṇaḥ prajñātmā | ānando'jaro'mṛtaḥ | na sādhunā karmaṇā bhūyān no evāsādhunā kanīyān ||3.8||
verses.english_translation: This prana, whose Self is consciousness, is bliss, ageless, immortal. It is not increased by good action, nor decreased by bad action.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — प्रज्ञात्मा — आनन्दः — अजरः — अमृतः। न साधुना भूयान् — न असाधुना कनीयान् — निर्विकारः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana (Brahman) is bliss, ageless, and immortal. It is not increased by good karma nor decreased by bad karma — it is beyond all action and its results. This is the nature of the Self — unchanging, eternal, beyond all modification.

verses.sanskrit: एष लोकाधिपतिः | एष लोकपालः | एष लोकेशः | स म आत्मेति विद्यात् ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: eṣa lokādhipatiḥ | eṣa lokapālaḥ | eṣa lokeśaḥ | sa ma ātmeti vidyāt ||3.8||
verses.english_translation: This is the lord of the worlds, the protector of the worlds, the ruler of the worlds. Know that as my Self.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — प्राणः — लोकाधिपतिः — लोकपालः — लोकेशः। स मे आत्मा — इति विद्यात्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana (Brahman) is the lord, protector, and ruler of all worlds. Indra tells Pratardana: 'Know that as my Self.' The Self of Indra, the Self of Pratardana, and the Self of all beings is the same prana-Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: स एव मे प्रियतमः | स एव मे श्रेष्ठः | स एव मे ज्येष्ठः | स एव मे वसिष्ठः ||३.२||
verses.transliteration: sa eva me priyatamaḥ | sa eva me śreṣṭhaḥ | sa eva me jyeṣṭhaḥ | sa eva me vasiṣṭhaḥ ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: He alone is my most dear, he alone is my best, he alone is my greatest, he alone is my most excellent.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सः — प्राणः — मे प्रियतमः — श्रेष्ठः — ज्येष्ठः — वसिष्ठः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Indra declares that prana (Brahman) is his most dear, best, greatest, and most excellent. This is the devotional expression of the Kaushitaki — the Self is the most beloved of all, for it is the source of all love and all value.

verses.sanskrit: स य एवं विद्वान् प्राणमुपास्ते | स सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानमेव पश्यति | सर्वेषु भूतेषु चात्मानम् ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: sa ya evaṃ vidvān prāṇam upāste | sa sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmānam eva paśyati | sarveṣu bhūteṣu cātmānam ||3.1||

word_meanings.meaning: sees all beings as the Self

verses.english_translation: One who knows thus and meditates on prana sees all beings as the Self alone, and the Self in all beings.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणम् उपास्ते — ब्रह्म उपास्ते — सः सर्वाणि भूतानि — आत्मानम् एव पश्यति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who meditates on prana as Brahman sees all beings as the Self and the Self in all beings. This is the vision of unity — the same teaching as the Isha Upanishad. Knowledge of Brahman leads to seeing the Self everywhere.

verses.sanskrit: स वा एष महान् अज आत्मा | योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु | य एषोऽन्तर्हृदय आकाशः | तस्मिञ्छेते ||३.२||
verses.transliteration: sa vā eṣa mahān aja ātmā | yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu | ya eṣo'ntarhṛdaya ākāśaḥ | tasmiñ chete ||3.2||

word_meanings.meaning: made of consciousness, among the pranas

verses.english_translation: This is the great unborn Self — made of consciousness, among the pranas — which rests in the ether within the heart.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: महान् अजः आत्मा — विज्ञानमयः — प्राणेषु — हृदयाकाशे — शेते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The great unborn Self — made of pure consciousness — rests in the ether of the heart. This is the same teaching as the Chandogya and Brihadaranyaka — the Self dwells in the heart as the innermost reality, smaller than the smallest yet greater than the greatest.
